game.title: Paralunar
game.short_text: Moon's haunted! Bust some ghosts in low gravity!

details.title: Paralunar
details.description:
GET SPOOKED! 
Moon's haunted, but Alex Gunn: Ghostbuster isn't going to let a few hundred thousand kilometers get in the way of what she does best: GHOST BUSTING.
Made for the GitHub Game Off 2020 in a month, Paralunar is a 2D shooting game set inside a haunted castle on the Moon. Shoot, float, dash and bounce around in low gravity while exorcising ghosts and other vengeful spirits!
EXPERIMENTAL WEB BUILD: Please download game for a more stable experience
Controls:
Shoot: Left mouse clickDash: Right mouse click / SpaceBrake: FMove left/right (on floor): A, DPause: StartQuit: EscFullscreen: F11
- Gamepad support is untested, but some players report that it works. - Aiming with the mouse doesn't work while a gamepad is connected. Please disconnect your gamepad/joystick when playing with mouse and keyboard!
Tip: Shoot the monoliths to save!Note: Dying loads the last saved game, so if you start a new game and die before saving, the game will load your last savefile.
 Consider making a donation if you liked the game :)

game.short_text: A competitive multiplayer space flight experience, played over a Twitch stream.
game.title: Launch Party

details.title: Launch Party
details.description:
As of January 2021, Launch Party is no longer streaming 24/7. You can still play it by downloading the executable and hosting your own stream.
In space, no one can hear you stream.

About
Launch Party is a multiplayer ship-programming game played via Twitch stream. Write some code, send it as a comment, and watch your ship take off! Everyone is racing to be the first to land on the moon.
Since it's a party game, it's recommended you play this game with three or more people. It's playable in any browser that supports Twitch, as well as on mobile with the Twitch app.
This game was made for the 2020 Github Game Off with the theme "Moonshot." All game programming, art, music, and sound design were completed within the one-month jam.
The entire game's source code is available in our Github repository. Feel free to use and distribute it in any way allowed by the MIT license.
Credits
plasmastarfish: programming, art, music, sound effectssuperduperpacman42: programmingFonts used:A Goblin AppearsASAPEras Demi ITCPixel Caps
Changelogs
12/4/2020: Fixed a bug where certain improper commands crashed the serverDownload and extract the game files.Open config.yaml (in the "config" folder) in a text editor like Notepad. Change the text after "channel: " from plasmastarfish to the Twitch account you'll be streaming the game from.Run LaunchParty.exe (in the "executable" folder).Start streaming the game to Twitch. If you don't already have streaming software you're familiar with, I recommend OBS.Once the stream is running, you're set! Follow the directions on the screen and send chat messages to play the game with your viewers.

game.short_text: City building for humanity's preservation (30 min)
game.title: Moonkind

details.title: Moonkind
details.description:
Take part in this 700 years long travel through interstellar space. Your ship isn't made out of metal, but is the Moon itself. You and all personnel inside the Moonbase are the only humans left in this universe, violentely propeled out of the solar system. Surviving in the void will require determination, ingenuity and strong management.
This is humanity's last hope, we're counting on you !
This game was made in 1 month for the Game Off 2020.
How to play ?
Build factories, housing and culture buildings by selecting them in the build menu and clicking on an available spot on the Moon.To start waiting, click on the Play or Play Fast button on the top right corner.Keep an eye on your population and your citizen's moodClick on a building to see its details, start or stop production, repair it, choose a project...
Small screen ? Click on the little arrows on the bottom right corner to make the game go fullscreen !
Tips and tricks
Start your game with a geothermal plant, an ice melter and a mine, or you'll quickly be out of resources !Empty factories can cost more than they can produce, keep an eye on your population and unemployement level before building a new geothermal plant.Don't be afraid to destroy buildings, if your population suddently dropped, you won't need that many housing districts !Birth/Death rate is affected by your citizen's mood, so try and keep them happy.Sometime, killing some folks is the only option...Population will plateau after a certain point, but you can still grow with research in medecine or night clubs.
Credits
Awesome sfx work from Alessandra URSO
Tools used : Unity, Blender, Piskel, FL Studio.
Moon model from Turbosquid

SUPPORT ME

game.short_text: First-person fantasy action! Cut down armies of undead on your way to save the world from the evil LICH.
game.title: BLOODMOON

details.title: BLOODMOON
details.description:
"One thousand years ago, the powerful LICH NUR ravaged the world with his ARMIES OF THE DEAD.But the mighty PALADIN defeated him, and banished him to CASTLE BLOODMOON, a cursed fortress that exists only during the red light of a LUNAR ECLIPSE.The Earth prospered! But as the centuries passed, LICH NUR's power grew from within his purgatory, and every BLOODMOON he raised his ARMIES OF THE DEAD to terrorize the world of the living once more.As the BLOODMOON TERROR grew, the world of the living needed a MOONSHOT SOLUTION; a new plan to end this horror once and for all.So they turned to me, WIZARD NODROG, to raise the PALADIN from the dead, that he might reopen CASTLE BLOODMOON, and destroy the LICH NUR forever.And that, PALADIN, is how you come to be alive again, on this night of the BLOODMOON.  Will you help us?  Will you end this BLOODMOON TERROR?"
- The Wizard, Nodrog

In BLOODMOON is an early-90s DOS-era-inspired first person action adventure.  You pay as a warrior known only as "The Paladin", who has been resurrected by the descendants of the people he once saved to finally destroy his old nemesis, Lich Nur.  Battle through three levels and a boss fight to uncover the story and save the world.

Built for Github Game Jam 2020 by Phil Breczinski.  This is my first game jam, and my first game in about 20 years.WINDOWS:
Download both the .exe and .pck file and place them both in the same folder. Run the .exe file to play!
MAC:
Download the .dmg file and double click it to open.  Run the "Bloodmoon" executable within to play!

moreInfo.Author: LiveOVErdrive
moreInfo.Tags: Fantasy, First-Person, My First Game Jam, Swords

game.short_text: A mad scientist slashes through floating remnants of civilization in a desperate, mysterious quest to reach the moon.
game.title: Skyjunk

details.title: Skyjunk
details.description:
Move - Left stick / WASDAim - Right stick / mouseSlice - Right trigger / left clickHook - Left trigger / right clickJump, Reel In - Left shoulder / Space
Gamepad recommended, M&K supported.
Jump, swing, and fly through a unique landscape of floating ships and platforms from a once-functioning civilization, still guarded by vigilant robots. On each stage gather enough scrap from boxes, barrels -- and the robots -- to repair and power your ship as it propels you ever-upwards to the moon.
(In this Gamejam demo, there is only one playable stage. Try to see if you can collect enough scrap to escape the unique-each-time level before the robots get you!)
code, 3d art, music, and sfx by gameshapedprocedural level generation by Aaron Gorensteindesign by both
Downloading and running the game should improve your framerate if it feels choppy in the browser.

It is a physics-based puzzle game with some interesting mechanics.

game.short_text: A game made for GitHub Game Jam 2020 with the theme of 'Moonshot'
game.title: Moon Liberator

details.title: Moon Liberator
details.description:
You are a normal person that does normal things,  that some how got held prisoner in some sort of science facility, where you have a strong desire to escape. Find your way out using a weapon that harnesses the power of the moon to blast your way through scientists, guards, mutants, and other enemies in order to see day light again.  If you end up failing, no worries, you will just wake up back in your cell ready to escape again. Maybe this time a bit more powerful.

CONTROLS:
Keyboard:
WASD / ZQSD / Arrow keys = MoveF = KickSpace = JumpShift+Space = DashE = InteractLeft Mouse Button = Primary AttackRight Mouse Button = Secondary attack (hold to charge)Tab = Open MinimapM = Toggle Music
Game Pad (Xbox):
Left stick = MoveB = KickA = JumpLT + A = DashRB = InteractX = Primary attack facing player directionY = Secondary attack facing player direction (hold to charge)Right stick = Free Aim with primary attackLT + Right stick = Free Aim with secondary attack (hold to charge)DPad Up = Open MinimapSelect = Toggle Music
This game was created during the month of November in 2020 for the GitHub Game Off game jam.  The code repository can be found here.
It is HIGHLY recommended to play with mouse and keyboard due to the game relying on aiming with a mouse. Game pads are fully supported but is much more difficult to aim. But feel free to use whichever suits you best.
A full play-through  on reaching and defeating the boss should take about an hour or so depending on luck and skill.

Music and sound effects by Patran.
Art by Annahvi.

Young Light, one of the characters from my upcoming game Neon Night, has found himself on a moon and some bats are suddenly attacking him. He has nothing but himself to survive!

The game is a fast paced arcade where you must stay on a rotating moon while combating enemies that increase in numbers and types.

game.short_text: Shoot goo to terraform the moon!
game.title: Goona

details.title: Goona
details.description:
Try to tackle the vast challenge of terraforming the moon by shooting genetically engineered super slime at it and have it multiply.
Backstory
The earth is becoming uninhabitable. The governments of the world are down to one drastic option; follow the advice of a mad scientist and spread generically engineered super slimes on the moon that they claim will terraform it into a utopia.Your job is to shoot the slimes from your ship according to the scientist's instructions.Good Luck.
InstructionsYou start each level with various slimes. To beat the level a certain number of tiles have to be filled by each slime as directed.Choose which slime to place from the selection at the bottom and click on the grid to place it. When you are ready you can start the slime's growth by clicking the start button to the right of the selection panel. (Slime can be placed after you click start as well.)The slimes will spread out according to their growth conditions. The objective is to get a specific minimum of every slime available to you before the time runs out, shown on the display on the left.

moreInfo.Tags: 2D, Casual, Non violent, Sci-fi, Short, Singleplayer, Space, Top-Down, Unity

game.title: Shoot the Moon (like literally)
game.short_text: sci-fi puzzle matching game with a story

details.title: Shoot the Moon (like literally)
details.description:
You landed a job as a destruction sequence manager. Your job: find the proper sequence to fire the Moonerator laser to destroy the moons. Find the perfect sequence for each moon!
In this puzzle game, you fire a laser to destroy neighboring colors in a moon. The remaining blocks fall toward the center of the moon until you've completely destroyed the moon. Each moon must be solved within a certain number of shots.
Throughout its 55 levels, the game tells the story of the Moonerator's universe, and introduces the player to the galactic politics that forced the destruction of so many moons.

When Mira arrives back at the cabin now abandoned, searching for clues among memories and manuscripts, she finds an unexpected friend in the form of a bunny with a fuzzy black coat. After speaking to it about her troubles, she realizes that it isn't just the bunny who has been listening...

game.short_text: you are a self-aware vacuum cleaner trying a better life
game.title: Scrap Out

details.title: Scrap Out
details.description:
I recommend playing in full screen, some features - like the launch of the head - are very compromised without it
you are a small self-conscious vacuum cleaner and wake up in the middle of a big scrapyard. if you want to get out of it alive, you will have to face many challenges and think about the time to risk or play safely.
how far could a vacuum cleaner dream?
Control and Tips
use W. A, S, D to move, SPACE to jump, E to shoot and ENTER to pause and see the upgrades menu.
aslo use Q to the head launch mode, and them use the mouse to click, drag and set the direction of the launch. in that mode you can pres E anytime to teleport to head location, or click and drag again to get more impulse.
and the J, K and L are shortcuts to the energy of Head, Bullets and Shield
how you spend the energy for power your gears and the scrap parts to upgrade your skills is the most important thing on the game
Made in 30 days for the Game Off Jam, using Godot Engine, Krita, Tilesetter and Rytmik

game.short_text: If you shoot for the stars, they might shoot back.
game.title: The First Wave

details.title: The First Wave
details.description:
Probe intercepted. Contents: a gold disc. Revealed: a planet in a distant galaxy. Your mission is to reach it. You are the first wave.
Ver 1.5 | Post Jam (Game-Off 2020) 
The game may have trouble loading when adblockers or pop-up blockers are enabled. If they are already off - please be patient! The game may take a little time to load.
Gameplay
Best experienced in full screen!
Pick and train recruits, each with their own perks and flaws  Stop by different planets on your mission to reach Earth. Some are barren rocks, others are teeming with wildlife.On planets with sentient life, develop and execute plans of action as a means to get what you need.Fight enemy spaceships, and master combat that involves three elementsUpgrade your crew and ship with research to build toward a specific playstyleMine metals from asteroids and hunt animals to collect biomassDiscover up to 5 endings in your encounters with the human race

Quick Look
Build a unique crew. Research and adapt to your playstyle

Command your crew in gathering excursions

Control your ship's weapon systems


Controls
WAD - TurnRight Mouse Button - ZoomH - Help / Pause
Credits
We are MergeConflict Studios.

Red Nur - Code, Music, 2D ArtCarlos Fernandez - 3D Art, Sound FX

game.short_text: Embark on a perilous mission in this first person platformer, turning foes into launchpads of freedom!
game.title: Cosmonauty

details.title: Cosmonauty
details.description:
Description:
Game Off 2020 Submission!
Embark on a lone, perilous, mission of Cadet Howard Moonshine in this satire/parody of the 20th century space race! Thrilling first person platformer. Jump, shoot, wall-run, and dash to traverse heights and obstacles by turning foes into launchpads of freedom!
First Person Shooter & Platformer
Made in  30 days in Unreal Engine!

How to Play: 
W - A - S - D:  Movement 
Mouse Movement:  Look Around / Aim
Spacebar:  Jump
Hold Space (while in midair):  Slow Decent/Hover 
Shift:  Dash
Left Mouse Button:  (LMB) Shoot
Hold Left Mouse Button (LMB):  Charge Shot
Hold Right Mouse Button (RMB):  Aim Down Sight
Jump into Blue Panels and Hold Forward:  Wall-Run
F11:  Toggle Fullscreen/Windowed
Gameplay is also compatible with a controller, but the menu system currently requires a mouse!

Credits: 
Chris - Code (UE4 Blueprint), Compiler, Systems Designer, Sound Implementation, AI, UI/UX Design, VFX
Austin Edwards - Code (UE4 Blueprint), Game Designer, Technical & VFX Artist, Level Design, Sound Implementation, UI/UX Design
Nathan Pierce - Environment Artist, Lighting, Level Design, 3D Modeling, Texturing, Coding (UE4 Blueprint), Technical Artist
Kevin Chung - Character Artist, 3D Prop Artist, Texturing 
Alex Cote - Composer, SFX Designer/Artist 
Robert Cote - Voice Over Artist
Kate Jones - Graphic Designer, UI Illustrator,  Animator

Install to Play Instructions: 

Just download, unzip, and double-click the .EXE icon and play!

Updates to Page Since Submission:
- Updated a few roles to the credits
- Added Chris as a collaborator!
- Updated description
- Implemented a fix for a bug that could occur during the final boss, preventing completion of the game.
- Added "Wall-Run" to controls
- More screenshots!

game.short_text: GlassTombs
game.title: Glass Tombs

details.title: Glass Tombs
details.description:
A short STORY-FOCUSED adventure with a  DYNAMIC 8 SONG SOUNDTRACK that takes place in a unique take on the world of an ACTION RPG.
(Made completely from scratch in Eclipse. No engine used)
A moonshot is an extremely ambitious and innovative endeavor. The Glass Dungeon is a dungeon that no adventurer has ever completed. Our player, who has never walked the dark halls of a dungeon, decides to take on the challenge of the impossible Glass Dungeon.

"A elegant building made of pristine glass... where countless adventurer's lives were ended.
To accomplish this, the player will travel to 3 of the surrounding crypts and talk to the ghosts of heroes who have died attempting the Glass Dungeon. 

"Townspeople build crypts to honor fallen heroes."
Every area in the world has been randomly generated. Before setting foot in the glass dungeon, the player can talk to people in town, might have to fight some enemies along the way in the forest, use their map to help them navigate the unique world.
OFFICIAL SOUNDTRACKhttps://soundcloud.com/thisisyzzy/sets/glass-tombs-official-soundtrackWe collaborated and made the soundtrack complete with 8 tracks.The soundtrack is dynamic and changes while you play the game:All the instruments in 'Overworld' go silent except for the drums when you are outside of  a crypt.The 'Town' and  'House' themes switch seamlessly into each other.

NOTE FROM THE DEVELOPER:This month contained my roommate testing positive, me moving back home from campus to quarantine, and my girlfriend moving back home states away.As how hectic this month was, I loved being in the grind. This whole month I was doing what I love with the people I love.My girlfriend made all the music and my dad helped me revise the story.I would greatly appreciate a play and a rating but even a comment would be amazing. Thank you all so so so very much.Windows computers will have it work by default. Just click on the executable. Don't move it out of the folder or you will lose the fonts but it would still work.MAC INSTRUCTIONS:
If if you are on the Mac version and it says it can't open even after the security check, don't worry about it:
Drag the folder to your desktopOpen Terminal (in the search bar)type:cd desktopcd Glass Tombs OSXjava -jar GlassTombs.jarIf an 'exception' occurs. Download this.

game.title: Moonarchists
game.short_text: Planetary pool game where you are a moon shooting moons at other moons

details.title: Moonarchists
details.description:
The rebel moonarchists created a moon replicator that can shoot mini-moons and they decided that it's time to take over their orbital systems.
Shoot mini-moons at other planets to push them to their doom and be the last celestial body in orbit!
NOTE: The HTML5 version may run too slow on older computer, so it's better to try one of the native versions.

game.title: Moonglove
game.short_text: 2D baseball video game on the moon

details.title: Moonglove
details.description:
Moonglove
2D baseball video game on the moon. The aliens challenge you to hit the ball as hard as possible to reach all the bases before the ball hits the moon.  If you win, you will receive a moonglove.
You have to move between the celestial objects like in Super Mario Galaxy.The levels will increase in difficulty adding obstacles between the bases. X-Y minutes playtime.Game made in 1 month for Game Off 2020.Theme:  'Moonshot'
Controls




Credits

Game made by @SimpleandClean_ and @Raulserranodev.
Music by: @SimpleandClean_.

game.short_text: Fight Moon with a spaceship and guns!
game.title: Battle Ship

details.title: Battle Ship
details.description:
The nuclear war tore the space and rule of physics, destroyed 70% of the Earth and caused the Moon to mutate into a magical monster. As one of few people left, pilot the spaceship, carry the last few guns on the Earth, go to the universe to fight against the Moon, you are the last hope of mankind.
Controls
W - fire minigun
A/D - fire pistol
S - fire rifle
R+R - reloading
Left Mouse Click - fire shotgun
This game was created for the GitHub Game Off game jam.
Music by Eric Matyas www.soundimage.org
Moon art from https://helianthus-games.itch.io/pixel-art-planets
Please download PC build for better sound quality.

moreInfo.Author: quittingJack
moreInfo.Tags: 2D, Pixel Art, Shoot 'Em Up, Top down shooter

game.short_text: Take on the role of one of the Roman Gods and become the Golf God champion by shooting the moon into a black hole.
game.title: Golfing Gods

details.title: Golfing Gods
details.description:
Golfing Gods
The Roman Gods used to look envious towards the Greek Gods' Olympic Games. Thus, they decided to host their own competitions.
However, theirs ought to be much more exciting! Instead of using dull earthly objects to throw, the Roman Gods shoot the moon around planets, aiming for a black hole.
From now on, the Golf Games are hosted every four years. The best gods compete. Each god is allowed to use one of their godly powers in the contest.
Take on the role of one of the Roman Gods and become the Golf God Champion.
Controls
SPACECharge the moon while holding, shoot the moon on releaseRRestart LevelEStart your God PowerMouse (left)Use your God PowerMouse (wheel)Zoom in and out  to view the galaxy
God Powers
Click the icon next to the God's name to see the power in action.
FortunaThe luck of Fortuna slows  down time and allows you to move one obstacle in the level (drag and drop).MarsBattle-scared Mars knows how to defend against damage. Next time the moon would crash into a planet, it bounces away instead.JupiterHis mighty lightning bolts are known everywhere. Give your moon a boost in a direction by dropping a lightning bolt (click) behind it.
Obstacles
        Planets
         The planet's gravitational force will attract the moon, which can help   you to reach your destination. However, be careful. If the moon is too close   to the planet's surface, it will explode.
             Waypoint Planets
         Waypoint planets allow you to throw the moon again.
             Asteroids
         Colliding with asteroids will not make the moon explode, but can   change the moon's course.
             Star
         Collect stars to unlock new gods to play.
             Black Hole
         The target where you should aim the moon at. 
Level Editor
Design your own levels and share them with your friends.  
Who can design the best levels and who can complete all of them? 
Share your creations with us for a chance to get featured in the next release. If we feature your level, we will add your name to the credits. 


Fixes after entry
v1.0.3: Fixed Text positions in Tutorial levels

moreInfo.Rating: Rated 4.0 out of 5 stars(1 total ratings)

game.short_text: Master gravity and slingshot planets into orbit! Made for the Game Off 2020 game jam.
game.title: Orbit

details.title: Orbit
details.description:
Instructions:Click and drag back to slingshot moons towards the planets, get enough moons into orbit to continue!Description:Created for Game Off 2020: Moonshot
Programing and music by Matt Hill.
Texture work by Larra Snyder.Postmortem - 6/24/2021    First game published using Unity. Up until this point I had been getting pretty comfortable with GameMaker Studios.    Learning from the mistakes of a few of my previous jams, We looked for an idea that had high potential, was unique, and didn't require too much programing work. We had a lot of time for this game jam, but a lot of it was taken up trying to organize workflow between us and figure out GitHub.    Graphics turned out ok I guess. Looking back at it we probably would have been better served by a more cartoony 2D aesthetic. The music for this was created last minute, and turned out ok, but I definitely should have made the loop longer. A 15 second background music loom quickly gets tiring.    Gameplay wise, this was an interesting idea, but boils down to guessing where your moons will go, as there is no good way to predict them, or to line up a similar shot to your previous one.    On the programming side, I had a lot of fun trying to "fix" gravity for this project. I needed to adjust the math to make it easier for the player to land things in orbit, as real world physics don't allow for it to work the way we need it to work.    Another improvement from previous entries, I made an official title screen and menu system. Had a lot of fun with the drop shadow on this.    This project placed very well in the rankings for this jam, I was very happy with my scores. However I still did not managed to get featured. I still have a ways to go to make my games more memorable.

game.title: Moon & Shot
game.short_text: A bullet hell kinda game

details.title: Moon & Shot
details.description:
Submission for Game Off 2020 WASD - Move AroundLeft-Click - Interact with the world / NPCRight-Click - Restructure spell formula / Cast a spell 
Theme : MoonshotTheme interpretation I describe 'Moonshot' as a form of casting a spell. The player need to restructure a spell formula before casting it. The casting formula is represent in a form of circle around the player which I describe as a 'Moon' and the act of activating the spell is what I call 'Shot'

game.short_text: Yuna travels around different trying to bring them back to her system in this intense time-crunched platformer!
game.title: Yuna and the Moonstealers

details.title: Yuna and the Moonstealers
details.description:
In Yuna and the Moonstealers you travel to different moons, place wells to help move around and rush back to the ship when your mission is complete! It's a fast paced, precision platformer!
Controls (gamepad w/d-pad is highly encouraged):

You can switch between Normal and Easy mode in-game. In Easy mode, you get extra wells to place in each room and there is no time limit on getting back to the ship.

game.short_text: A peaceful game of space photography
game.title: Void Idyllic

details.title: Void Idyllic
details.description:
Explore and photograph a petite solar system filled with ancient ruins and strange flora. A short game made for the GameOff 2020 game jam.
Recommended System
Windows 10 and a GTX 970 or better are recommended. 
FAQ
Q: Are my photographs saved anywhere?A: Yes! On windows, copies of all the photographs you take will be stored in your "Pictures\VoidIdyllic" folder. If you are using the Itch app's sandboxing feature, they will be stored in the itch-player user's Pictures folder instead.Q: My computer doesn't meet the recommended requirements; can I still run the game?A: Probably. On less powerful systems you may be able to improve performance by reducing the Particle Density option in the game settings, as those are the game's most demanding feature.Q: Will the game be available for other platforms, or as a web build?A: A web build is unlikely because of the game's size, but OSX and Linux builds may be available in the future.
Controls Reference
Most controls are taught by the in-game tutorial; this list is just for reference.
Keyboard/MouseGamepadNavigate (Menu)WASD/Arrow KeysLeft StickSelect (Menu)Spacebar/Left Mouse (Point+Click)A (lower face button)Toggle MenuTab/EscapeY (upper face button)/StartMove (Flying or On Foot)WASD/Arrow KeysLeft StickTurn (Flying or On Foot)Move MouseRight StickRoll (Flying)Q/ELeft/right TriggersAscend/Descend (Flying)Spacebar/CtrlA/B (lower/right face buttons)Jump and Hover (On Foot)SpacebarA (lower face button)Aim Camera (Flying or On Foot)Right Mouse (Hold)Left Bumper (Hold)Take Photo (While Aiming)    (Not available in menus)Left Mouse (Click)Right BumperRotate camera (While Aiming or in Menu)Q/ELeft/Right Triggers
Credits
Design, Art and Code: Thomas MorleyMusic and Sound Design: James Evans (jamesmusic.co.uk)
